us_common_core_grade: ages 12-13 (Common Core State Standards Initiative; CCSSO grade alignment)

Grade-level standards published at grade-level granularity only. Each standard is expected to be mastered by the end of the named grade. Sub-grade progression (quarterly, monthly) is a pedagogical decision made by districts and schools, not by the standards themselves. The presence of a single band in this reference output reflects the source's own granularity, not an absence of internal structure within the teaching year.

column4: Grade 7 learners extend proportional reasoning from Grades 5-6 to analyse proportional relationships using equations, tables, and graphs, and apply them to multi-step ratio and percent problems. This domain is foundational for linear algebra and functional thinking in Grade 8.
I can compute unit rates from ratios of quantities, including fractions and different units.
I can explain what a unit rate is and how it relates to ratios of quantities.
column10: I can explain how a unit rate describes the relationship between two quantities in a ratio.

## source_type: cluster_02: Recognizing and Representing Proportional Relationships
column4: I can identify whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship by testing equivalent ratios or analyzing graphical representations.
column8: blk_0002_item_01; blk_0007_item_01; blk_0007_item_02; blk_0009_item_01; blk_0009_item_02; blk_0009_item_03
column10: I can identify proportional relationships by testing equivalent ratios or analyzing graphs.

## source_type: cluster_02: Recognizing and Representing Proportional Relationships
column4: I can represent proportional relationships using multiple mathematical forms including tables, graphs, equations, and other representations.
column10: I can represent proportional relationships using tables, graphs, and equations.

## source_type: cluster_03: Identifying and Using the Constant of Proportionality
column4: I can identify the constant of proportionality across tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions.
column10: I can identify the constant of proportionality from tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions.

## source_type: cluster_03: Identifying and Using the Constant of Proportionality
column3: Using the Constant in Proportional Equations
column4: I can use the constant of proportionality to write and interpret equations and ordered pairs in proportional relationships.
column10: I can use the constant of proportionality to write equations representing proportional relationships.

## source_type: cluster_04: Solving Real-World Proportional Relationship Problems
column3: Solving Multistep Ratio and Percent Problems
column4: I can solve multistep ratio and percent problems including simple interest, tax, markups, markdowns, gratuities, commissions, fees, percent increase and decrease, and percent error.

## source_type: cluster_04: Solving Real-World Proportional Relationship Problems
column3: Applying Proportional Relationships to Real-World Contexts
column4: I can apply proportional relationships to solve real-world and mathematical problems involving multiple steps and reasoning.
column10: I can solve multi-step real-world problems by applying proportional relationships and reasoning about the results.
